hypothesis.thesis_statement: Paper 'Capturing dynamics of post-earnings-announcement drift using genetic algorithm-optimised supervised learning' (arXiv:2009.03094) proposes a signal in the 'earnings_events' dimension. Hypothesis: an A-share adaptation predicts the cross-section of forward returns. Test whether the published effect ports to A-shares.
hypothesis.mechanism: Taxonomy dimension: earnings_events (FRONTIER_OPEN). Buildable on: earnings_preannounce(forecast/express); fundamentals(income/balancesheet/cashflow/pit_fundamentals/indicators). Abstract: While Post-Earnings-Announcement Drift (PEAD) is one of the most studied stock market anomalies, the current literature is often limited in explaining this phenomenon by a small number of factors using simpler regression methods. source.title: Capturing dynamics of post-earnings-announcement drift using genetic algorithm-optimised supervised learning

pre_registered_concerns.most_likely_failure_mode: [DRAFT] The paper's effect may not survive A-share microstructure (T+1, limit boards, retail flow) or may be subsumed by existing factors after neutralization.
pre_registered_concerns.weakest_assumption: [DRAFT] That the paper's construction maps faithfully to Tushare A-share fields without definitional drift.
pre_registered_concerns.what_would_falsify_this: [DRAFT] Sealed-OOS rank-ICIR below the committed band, or hard rules failing after realistic costs.
pre_registered_concerns.priors_on_cost_sensitivity: [DRAFT] Review turnover at the chosen rebalance.

arxiv_evidence_context_only.abstract: While Post-Earnings-Announcement Drift (PEAD) is one of the most studied stock market anomalies, the current literature is often limited in explaining this phenomenon by a small number of factors using simpler regression methods. In this paper, we use a machine learning based approach instead, and aim to capture the PEAD dynamics using data from a large group of stocks and a wide range of both fundamental and technical factors. Our model is built around the Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) and uses a long list of engineered input features based on quarterly financial announcement data from 1,106 companies in the Russell 1000 index between 1997 and 2018. We perform numerous experiments on …

Run the SANDBOX screen + size/industry-neutralized MARGINAL-contribution test vs the catalog (marginal IC × low correlation — the house rule), NOT standalone ICIR.
SET expected_effect = YOUR A-share prediction (point + CI + horizon).
CONFIRM the OOS window is unburned, then factor_lifecycle (draft→candidate) → sealed-OOS.
